step_name: Implement NIST AI RMF - Measure and Manage

subprocess_flow.description: The AI Architect initiates the measurement phase by configuring automated risk monitoring and quantification tools. This involves setting up technical parameters to track model performance, data quality, and security vulnerabilities in real-time, ensuring that the measurement process is consistent and scalable across the financial institution's AI portfolio.
subprocess_flow.purpose: To establish a technical foundation for continuous and automated risk quantification.
subprocess_flow.scope: Technical configuration of risk monitoring software and integration with existing model pipelines.
triggers: Completion of NIST AI RMF Govern and Map phase.
end_conditions: Risk measurement tools are fully integrated and reporting data.

step_risks_and_controls.risk: Incompatible tool integration with legacy financial systems.
step_risks_and_controls.control: Pre-deployment API compatibility testing and sandbox validation.

change_management.change_request_process: Standard IT change request for tool configuration updates.
change_management.versioning_rules: Configuration scripts stored in version-controlled repositories.

subprocess_flow.description: Using the reports from the 'Govern and Map' phase, the AI Architect applies quantitative methods to measure the impact and likelihood of identified risks. This includes stress testing models against financial market volatility and assessing the potential severity of bias or privacy breaches using standardized metrics.
subprocess_flow.purpose: To provide a data-driven assessment of risk levels for prioritization.
subprocess_flow.scope: Quantitative analysis of all risks mapped in the previous phase.
end_conditions: Risk scores are assigned to all identified AI risks.

step_risks_and_controls.risk: Inaccurate risk quantification due to poor data quality.
step_risks_and_controls.control: Data integrity checks and statistical validation of measurement models.

governance_requirements: EU AI Act Risk Assessment Standards

change_management.change_request_process: Peer review of quantification methodology changes.
change_management.versioning_rules: Methodology documents versioned in the compliance portal.

subprocess_flow.description: The AI Architect designs specific technical and procedural controls to mitigate risks that exceed the organization's risk appetite. This includes implementing differential privacy layers, enhancing model explainability modules, and establishing automated 'kill-switches' for models that exhibit unintended behavior in production environments.
subprocess_flow.purpose: To reduce AI risk to acceptable levels through active management.
subprocess_flow.scope: Development and deployment of technical risk mitigations.
end_conditions: Mitigation controls are active and verified.

step_risks_and_controls.risk: Mitigation controls negatively impacting model accuracy.
step_risks_and_controls.control: Performance-impact trade-off analysis during control design.

change_management.change_request_process: Formal approval from the AI Ethics Committee for new controls.
change_management.versioning_rules: Control logic versioned alongside model code.

subprocess_flow.description: The AI Architect compiles all measurement data and management actions into the final NIST AI RMF Measure & Manage Report. This document provides a comprehensive audit trail of how risks were quantified and what specific actions were taken to manage them, serving as a critical deliverable for regulatory compliance.
subprocess_flow.purpose: To document the risk management process for internal and external stakeholders.
subprocess_flow.scope: Compilation of all measurement data, mitigation actions, and residual risk assessments.
end_conditions: NIST AI RMF Measure & Manage Report is signed off by the Head of AI Governance.

step_risks_and_controls.control: Independent review of the report by the Risk Management Team.

change_management.change_request_process: Document revision process via the compliance management system.
change_management.versioning_rules: Major versioning for final reports; minor versioning for drafts.
